falsification_conditions.condition: The price closes above the 200-day moving average by at least 5% within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This would indicate a strong bullish trend contradicting the 'HOLD' signal and suggesting the pattern is not a blowoff but a continuation of an uptrend.

falsification_conditions.condition: Volume spikes to 200% above the 30-day average on a single day with a price increase of >3%
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Such a surge would signal massive buying pressure, invalidating the 'UNKNOWN' verdict by confirming a clear bullish breakout rather than a potential blowoff.

falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 breaks above its all-time high by 4% within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This would directly contradict the blowoff scenario, proving the current pattern is not a topping formation but part of a broader bullish cycle.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
